item.title: ITI formalises recruitment partnership with Lae corporate houses
item.description: The International Training Institute [ITI] has formalised a partnership with five major corporate houses in Lae, Morobe, to secure direct employment and training pathways for its graduates. The institution signed separate Memorandums of Understanding [MoUs] with the Lae Biscuit Company, SVS, Frabelle Fisheries, CBS Tech, and the Lae Travelers Inn last week.
item.content:encoded:
The International Training Institute [ITI] has formalised a partnership with five major corporate houses in Lae, Morobe, to secure direct employment and training pathways for its graduates.

The institution signed separate Memorandums of Understanding [MoUs] with the Lae Biscuit Company, SVS, Frabelle Fisheries, CBS Tech, and the Lae Travelers Inn last week.

Under the terms of the three-year agreement, the companies will provide immediate on-the-job training placements for ITI students upon the completion of their studies, creating a direct pipeline into the workforce.

ITI chairman and co-founder Senthil Kumaran Sentheyval said while the institution had informally placed students with these corporate partners for years, the signing is part of a shift towards structured institutional cooperation.

"This has been a practice for quite some time, but it is made official now with the companies," Sentheyval said. "We will make sure we do the same for the other companies who take in our students for job training."

Sentheyval said ITI's curriculum remains strictly aligned with industry needs, ensuring graduates transition seamlessly into corporate environments.

"We train our students to be compatible with the workforce, which ITI has a track record of. Companies do not hesitate to make recruitments from us," he said.

Speaking during the presentation, Lae Biscuit Company human resources manager Peter Powih commended the initiative but challenged the prospective trainees to prioritise professional integrity over academic merit alone.

"Being knowledgeable and skilled is one thing, but having a good character makes a person stand out among others," Powih said. "I encourage students to conduct themselves well in the office, having respect for themselves and their peers in any working environment."

Industry representatives at the ceremony indicated that students would be absorbed through structured quotas or mass recruitment intakes. Trainees will undergo rigorous performance evaluations during their probationary period, with high-performing individuals transitioned into permanent, full-time staff.

Meanwhile, Sentheyval has issued an open invitation to other corporate stakeholders and employers in the Momase region to partner with the institute to expand the program.

item.title: BU energises Rosso bike race with K25,000
item.description: Coca-cola Europacific Partners Papua New Guinea [CCEP PNG] has backed the 2026 Rosso Mountain Bike Race and Lae Festival with a K25,000 gold sponsorship under its BU Energy Drink brand. The sponsorship package was presented to the event’s organizing committee in Lae yesterday, ahead of the two-day event which cues off this weekend [June 20-21].
item.content:encoded:
Coca-cola Europacific Partners Papua New Guinea [CCEP PNG] has backed the 2026 Rosso Mountain Bike Race and Lae Festival with a K25,000 gold sponsorship under its BU Energy Drink brand.

The sponsorship package was presented to the event’s organizing committee in Lae yesterday, ahead of the two-day event which cues off this weekend [June 20-21].

Event representative John Byrne acknowledged CCEP PNG's long-standing commitment to the community event, which is now into its fifth year.

"The Coca-Cola company has been supporting this initiative for the past five years, and this year they have supported us again," Byrne said. "We appreciate such a gesture from a company that prioritizes its community obligation."

Byrne revealed that rider participation has scaled up significantly this year, with a total of 1,700 cyclists registered to compete across the two days.

"We have increased participation to 1,700 riders; comprising 700 adults and 1,000 children. They will be racing in different heats with the finals set for Sunday," he said.

"On Sunday, we have children between the ages of seven and 12 having their separate races.

"The event has also been made conducive for people with special needs. We have created a very safe and healthy environment for them to take part in two separate events on Sunday."

CCEP PNG representative Serah Moana said the BU Energy brand naturally aligned with the core spirit of the competition.

"BU is synonymous with energy, endurance, and performance," Moana said. "We are proud to support not only the race but the festive program that runs parallel with it. It is a cornerstone cultural celebration delivered by the Lae City Authority, showcasing the richness and diversity of PNG’s traditions through music, dance, food, and community engagement."

As part of the gold sponsorship package, BU Energy Drink will run high-impact on-ground experiences, including branded activation zones and product sampling for athletes and spectators over the weekend.

item.title: Bogia police get support from locals
item.description: Bogia police can count themselves lucky this week after receiving two separate donations from Yawar Local Level Government and Manam Resettlement Authority. Yawar LLG presented a brand-new diesel generator to help light up the Bogia Police Station at night as well as help in their administration work, whilst MaRA gave them new sets of uniform.
item.content:encoded:
Bogia police can count themselves lucky this week after receiving two separate donations from Yawar Local Level Government and Manam Resettlement Authority.

Yawar LLG presented a brand-new diesel generator to help light up the Bogia Police Station at night as well as help in their administration work, whilst MaRA gave them new sets of uniform.

The presentations were done on Thursday at the Bogia Police Station.

The assistance by Yawar to support Bogia District police was made possible following grant assistance from the Ramu Nickel Project's royalty earnings of 2025.

President of Yawar LLG, Lakus Ruambi, handed over the new generator set with its accessories to the Bogia station commander, senior sergeant Albert Saui.

Ruambi, during the presentation ceremony, thanked the nickel-cobalt mine developer, Ramu NiCo Management [MCC] Limited, and the Madang Provincial Government for the benefit sharing in the royalty.

He said Yawar LLG received over K130,000 this year from the Ramu Nickel Project royalty grant assistance, which they are so thankful for and plan to share the funds wisely in the various socio-economic sectors.

He further clarified that the purchase of the new diesel generator set was just over K10,000

Sergeant Saui thanked Yawar leaders for supporting Bogia police.

"The new generator would greatly assist the police personnel at Bogia to do their administrative work efficiently, particularly in preparing arrests files and prosecution papers," he said. "Previously, it was too costly as they resorted to do paper work using outside service providers."

Law and Order Sector Chairman in the Bogia District Development Authority, James Sukua acknowledged Ruambi for considering Bogia Rural Police Command with the assistance.

Meanwhile, another presentation also took place at that time, which saw the Manam Resettlement Authority [MaRA) present brand-new uniforms to Bogia police.

The Chief Executive Officer of MaRA, Richard Baia, presented the uniforms to the police boss.

Baia explained said; "The new uniforms for Bogia police were purchased after Snr. Sergeant Saui and his police personnel decided to sacrifice their allowances from providing security during the Kumil bridge disaster earlier this year.

"Instead of paying the officers their daily allowances, the funds were saved and utilized to purchase their new uniforms," he said.

item.title: Morobe LLG station restores power after 25 years
item.description: Public servants and residents at the Morobe Local Level Government [LLG] station in Huon Gulf will now benefit from a steady power supply following 25 years of operating in the dark. The breakthrough comes after the Huon Gulf District Development Authority [DDA] Board funded a new K130,000 generator to restore power to the LLG headquarters.
item.content:encoded:
Public servants and residents at the Morobe Local Level Government [LLG] station in Huon Gulf will now benefit from a steady power supply following 25 years of operating in the dark.

The breakthrough comes after the Huon Gulf District Development Authority [DDA] Board funded a new K130,000 generator to restore power to the LLG headquarters.

Dona councillor Joe Gani said the prolonged lack of reliable electricity had severely hindered public service delivery, forcing officers to constantly travel to Lae for basic administrative tasks.

"With this new generator improving the power supply, public servants and government officers can now remain at the LLG station to do their jobs," Gani said. "This will also save local residents from traveling all the way to Lae to consult LLG officers."

The power restoration was part of a major rollout of five development projects officially launched in the area. The other projects included; two new police institutional houses to boost local security, a new banana boat and outboard motor for the Zaka Health Centre to assist with medical emergencies, the distribution of 20 bicycles to ward councillors across the LLG’s 20 wards to assist with community mobility and rollout of K200,000 in disaster relief assistance to communities hit by the March 2026 natural disasters.

A major technological highlight of the event was the introduction of a new CCTV Surveillance and Video Conferencing Connectivity Initiative. The project digitally links Morobe, Salamaua, and Wampar Rural LLGs directly with the Huon Gulf District Administration to strengthen communication, law and order coordination, and disaster response.

Minister for Community Development, Youth and Religion, and Member for Huon Gulf, Jason Peter, stated at the launch that his administration remains focused on practical service delivery.

"Development is not about promises; it is about delivering results that directly improve the lives of our people," he said. "Today's launch demonstrates our commitment to strengthening law and order, improving healthcare, restoring essential services, and ensuring that development reaches every ward and village."

Peter assured the community that the DDA will continue to collaborate with local leaders, government agencies, churches, and communities to sustain development across the district.

item.title: Port Moresby city security paramount
item.description: National Capital District Governor, Powes Parkop says security is a critical aspect at this time as more activities continue to take place, especially in the nation’s capital. Parkop says while ensuring all vital infrastructure and services are put in place, safety of citizens must also be considered.
item.content:encoded:
National Capital District Governor, Powes Parkop says security is a critical aspect at this time as more activities continue to take place, especially in the nation’s capital.

Parkop says while ensuring all vital infrastructure and services are put in place, safety of citizens must also be considered.

NCD Governor used the groundbreaking ceremony of the Kumul House earlier this week, highlighting they are in the forefront in ensuring security is maintained.

“On our part as the municipal government, we are putting in place all the infrastructure and services that are vital and we are working with the police to ensure that safety is number one.” Governor Parkop said.

He assured city residents and businesses that a lot has been invested in this area, and they will ensure security is not compromised.

“Security is not just important for everybody, at the personal level, corporate level, but it's equally important for our country.

“The national capital of our country cannot be overrun by lawlessness.

“We are here to maintain the highest level of peace and security, to guarantee the safety of our people and our residents.” Parkop said.

Governor Parkop said, his team will work to ensure that security is present so that investors who come here are at peace and they feel at home and part of the equation, and they can invest and make what must be their home.

item.title: Elections plans progressing with training and awareness programs
item.description: With the 2027 National Election fast approaching, the PNG Electoral Commission is prioritizing the update of the Electoral Roll. Acting Electoral Commissioner, Margaret Vagi, said the Commission is working to ensure that each individual voter must be on the voter roll.
item.content:encoded:
With the 2027 National Election fast approaching, the PNG Electoral Commission is prioritizing the update of the Electoral Roll.

Acting Electoral Commissioner, Margaret Vagi, said the Commission is working to ensure that each individual voter must be on the voter roll.

Ms Vagi said officials have begun training and raising awareness about the electoral roll update around the provinces.

“The three-phase exercise commenced last month with the verification of the roll to ensure names and number of eligible voters in each electorate.

“And into the second phase, we have the electoral roll that will be displayed for new voters whose names are not on the roll to come and provide their details,” the acting EC confirmed.

Ms Vagi says training workshops have been conducted in the capital city as well as in the regions for election managers and assistant election managers on fieldwork expectations.

“Fifty percent of that has gone to the provinces. 50 percent we're using it on the documents of big-ticket items like ballot boxes, seals, inks, the voting compartments.

“Claims have been processed, checks raised and then we should purchase some 50 percent of the big-ticket items for provinces.” Vagi said.

Ms Vagi also clarified that additional funding is needed for the third phase of the training for security and media personnel, including the six new electorates with their requirements.

item.title: Manalos aviation cadet program gets support from Lae City Authority
item.description: The Manalos Aviation Cadet Pilot Training Program has received its first direct support, with the Lae City Authority presenting K500,000 to help train young pilots from Lae. Lae City Authority Chief Executive Officer Robin Calistus presented the cheque on Thursday on behalf of Lae City Authority Chairman John Rosso.
item.content:encoded:
The Manalos Aviation Cadet Pilot Training Program has received its first direct support, with the Lae City Authority presenting K500,000 to help train young pilots from Lae.

Lae City Authority Chief Executive Officer Robin Calistus presented the cheque on Thursday on behalf of Lae City Authority Chairman John Rosso.

In presenting the cheque, Mr Calistus said the funding reflected the commitment of the Deputy Prime Minister and the Lae City Authority to supporting education, skills development, and opportunities for young Papua New Guineans in Lae.

“This cheque of K500,000, on behalf of the Deputy Prime Minister, Member for Lae and Chairman of the Lae City Authority, will support and help the Manalos Aviation in the cadet training programs,” Calistus said.

Manalos Aviation CEO Jurgen Ruh welcomed the support, describing it as the first time a government has directly assisted the institution’s pilot training efforts.

“This is the first time the PNG Government, or any government, has come forward to support us through pilot training. We have received support from the Lae City Authority and we are very thankful,” Ruh said.

He said financial challenges had previously slowed down the training of pilots and engineers, but the funding would now help increase training opportunities for more students.

“In the past, we had to conduct training at a slower pace due to cash flow challenges. With the support from the Lae City Authority, this funding will help us double and even triple our efforts to train more students in Lae at a faster rate. It means a lot to know that someone is supporting us,” he said.

Mr Ruh said the support would also benefit the country by helping train more Papua New Guineans in aviation.

“By helping us, you are helping Lae and the country. I have been training Papua New Guineans for 11 years and this is the first time I have received such support from the Government, especially the Lae District. Thank you very much, and hopefully other people will do the same,” he said.

The program has also reached another milestone with two female graduates from the Papua New Guinea University of Technology joining the cadet pilot training program.

Angela Wycliffe, who holds a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science, and Shiloh Tugula, who graduated with a Bachelor of Science in Applied Physics majoring in Electronic Instrumentation, are the first female participants in the cadet pilot program.

Both will begin training in July, starting with a course to obtain a private helicopter licence. The training will include aviation theory before progressing into commercial flight training towards full pilot certification.

Their inclusion marks an important step forward for women in aviation and highlights growing opportunities for female participation in Papua New Guinea’s aviation industry.

More young people from Lae are expected to join the cadet pilot training program in the future, provided they meet the requirements set by Manalos Aviation, like the first two young women, Shiloh and Angela.

The cadet pilot training program is fully funded by the taxpayers of Lae.

item.title: TISA Insurance donates vehicle to DBTI for automotive training
item.description: TISA Insurance has donated a salvaged vehicle to the Don Bosco Technological Institute [DBTI] to support the school's vocational automotive training program. The vehicle, which was previously involved in an accident, was assessed by the insurance company and deemed in suitable condition for educational use. Rather than scrapping the asset, the company opted to repurpose it as a instructional tool
item.content:encoded:
TISA Insurance has donated a salvaged vehicle to the Don Bosco Technological Institute [DBTI] to support the school's vocational automotive training program.

The vehicle, which was previously involved in an accident, was assessed by the insurance company and deemed in suitable condition for educational use. Rather than scrapping the asset, the company opted to repurpose it as a instructional tool for students learning modern automotive systems.

Vocational institutions frequently face resource constraints when attempting to provide students with hands-on experience on contemporary vehicles. DBTI faculty said access to intact, modern vehicle components helps bridge the gap between classroom theory and current industry standards, preparing students for the types of technology they will encounter after graduation.

According to a statement from TISA Insurance, the donation is part of an ongoing community initiative aimed at supporting local technical capacity and workforce development.

item.title: Former sprint champion spearheads Morata road upgrade project
item.description: Residents of Morata 3 in the Port Moresby North-West electorate are set to benefit from a community infrastructure service following the launch of a road maintenance and upgrade project along Nikints Street.

item.title: Works goes online with electronic payment system
item.description: The Works and Highways Department has launched its Electronic Funds Transfer [EFT] system and GoLive implementation on Tuesday, to replace the traditional paper checks.
